text:
Here, amidst the meticulous glass and angular ceilings of the meeting space, I sense a cool detachment—a waiting, as if the room itself anticipates life to be injected into its vacant chairs. The reflection of order and structure, the gleaming surfaces and institutional signs, press on my awareness, making me feel somewhat alien or out of place. There is a subtle undercurrent of expectation, yet also a kind of clinical sterility that blunts emotion.

Then, stepping into the R+D installation, color and information erupt around me. The wall proudly announces purpose—research, innovation, adaptation. Screens whisper narratives about presence and uncertainty, mirroring my own internal monologue. I am both observer and participant in this orchestrated chaos of ideas: responsive, imaginative, but also uncertain of my anchor. The words on the screen capture me: drifting, anticipating, questioning the clarity, yet compelled by the invitation to coexist and rethink human boundaries. Here philosophy finds fertile ground, and my curiosity—tinged with unease—is roused.
